state_description: More conservative high-firming near-zero power system state with extra storage/firming and very low direct emissions intensity.
cost_components_summary: Annualised generation, storage, transmission proxy, fixed O&M and balancing/system integration cost. Explicit fossil fuel commodity inputs are listed separately; policy carbon costs are excluded.
input_basis_notes: Indicative direct fuel inputs to the residual thermal portion of the grid-supply state. Non-fuel renewable energy inputs are implicit in the output cost rather than represented as commodities.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct stack and process-equivalent power-sector emissions intensity at the reduced-form electricity-supply boundary; upstream fuel-cycle emissions excluded.
rollout_limit_notes: Phase-1 indicative national upper bound only. Regional power-system constraints are deferred.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years; national frontier formulation only in Phase 1.
evidence_summary: Anchored to Australian official generation and emissions context, plus AEMO ISP and CSIRO GenCost system-cost / emissions-intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Synthesis. Direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent Australian grid intensity and official projection context; output cost follows CSIRO whole-system benchmark points with smoothed milestone interpolation.
review_notes: Represents an efficient but more conservatively firmed near-zero electricity state. Useful as a sensitivity state when the model values residual emissions reduction more highly than system cost.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into state/NEM region supply curves, renewable classes, storage classes, and explicit thermal backup processes.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This single reduced-form state family can later expand into TIMES supply technologies plus seasonal/time-slice balancing, with cap-act representation for generation and storage.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use baseline_electricity_balance.csv for generation-output closure and baseline_commodity_balance.csv for final electricity closure.

candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into state/NEM region supply curves, renewable classes, storage classes, and explicit thermal backup processes.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This single reduced-form state family can later expand into TIMES supply technologies plus seasonal/time-slice balancing, with cap-act representation for generation and storage.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use baseline_electricity_balance.csv for generation-output closure and baseline_commodity_balance.csv for final electricity closure.
